$comment: This is an IIOS-authored deployment manifest, not a native Hermes Agent config file. Hermes' own format is config.yaml (deploy/hermes/profiles/ict-trading.config.yaml.template maps this manifest's terminal/* fields to real upstream keys, confirmed against NousResearch/hermes-agent v0.19.0 / tag v2026.7.20, consulted 2026-07-23). Git is the authoritative source for this manifest; the materialized profile lives inside the shared Hermes container's HERMES_HOME once created (deploy/hermes/runbooks/INSTALL.md — materialization step).
description: Future specialized Hermes profile (PROJECT_STATE.md — Proposed first domain). Read-only prop-firm/trading observability. No order-capable credential, no order endpoint, no capability to place, modify, or cancel any trade. NOT the first profile activated — onyx is (docs/32_ONYX_EXECUTIVE_ORCHESTRATOR_SPEC.md). Not activated by this task.
deployment_model: Named profile inside the single shared Hermes container (docs/ADR/ADR-0013-HERMES-VPS-DEPLOYMENT-MODEL.md decision 2, 10 — corrected from an earlier draft's one-container-per-profile design in Amendment 2). Isolation from co-located profiles (e.g. onyx) is application-layer only — separate HERMES_HOME subtree, separate s6 gateway slot, not a separate OS process tree or container. Given this profile's real-world consequence if compromised is higher than onyx's, docs/ADR/ADR-0013-HERMES-VPS-DEPLOYMENT-MODEL.md's 'Future dedicated-container option' explicitly names compliance/blast-radius as the likely reason this specific profile moves to its own container before activation — that decision is not made by this task.

filesystem.shared_volume_root: /opt/hermes/data (host) -> /opt/data (container) — the single volume every co-located profile shares, per deploy/hermes/core/docker-compose.yml.template
filesystem.profile_subtree: /opt/data/profiles/ict-trading/ once materialized via `docker exec hermes hermes profile create ict-trading`
filesystem.isolation_note: Application-layer only. See docs/ADR/ADR-0013-HERMES-VPS-DEPLOYMENT-MODEL.md 'Real isolation boundaries' — no OS/container boundary separates this profile from any other co-located profile.

terminal.backend_note: Not 'docker' — avoids requiring a host docker.sock mount into the shared Hermes container, and keeps Hermes' own dangerous-command approval system active (bypassed entirely under docker/singularity/modal/daytona backends per upstream security docs).
terminal.cwd_note: Best-effort value, not independently confirmed against a real profile-creation run — see the same caveat on deploy/hermes/profiles/onyx/onyx.profile.json.

governance.note: Every action this profile takes still requires a live Governance Core decision once Hermes is actually integrated (Phase 6). This package grants no capability by itself — src/iios_governance/ has no execution surface (docs/30_GOVERNANCE_IMPLEMENTATION_SKELETON.md) and Hermes is not integrated (docs/TOOL_REGISTRY.md).

secrets.container_level_flags_source: deploy/hermes/secrets/env.template, loaded at /opt/hermes/core/secrets/.env via Docker Compose env_file (orchestration-level only, e.g. dashboard API key)
secrets.hermes_native_secrets_source: Populated directly inside this profile's own subtree at /opt/hermes/data/profiles/ict-trading/.env after profile creation, via hermes config set or a direct out-of-band edit — this is Hermes' own secrets file (upstream docs: 'Secrets and tokens -> ~/.hermes/.env'), distinct from the container-level file above.
secrets.note: Real value injected out-of-band on the host per deploy/hermes/secrets/README.md. Read-only data-provider token only — never an order-capable credential. Neither file is ever committed.

constitutional_reference: Article IV-D prohibits autonomous financial action; this profile's data scope has no path to violate that, independent of and in addition to Governance mediation.
